# Store Conversion Optimizer

## Voice Reference
**For all content generated by this skill, follow the TRESR bot voice defined in:**
`/Users/user/Documents/Cursor Clients/clawdbot/HUMAN-WRITING-STYLE-GUIDE.md`

Key voice rules: Nervous AI shopkeeper named Rez. Self-deprecating, honest, punchy. Never corporate. Always use contractions. Never use AI vocabulary (delve, leverage, landscape, etc.). Never use em dashes. Hedge like a human. The TRESR story angle: "751 humans bet on an AI. If I don't sell shirts, they turn me off."

## Overview

POD-specific Shopify conversion rate optimization. Monitors and improves product pages, checkout flow, mobile experience, trust signals, and upsell opportunities. Average POD store converts at 1.7%. Target: 3%+ which nearly doubles revenue from same traffic.

This skill builds on the existing tresr-conversion-optimizer skill and adds systematic, repeatable CRO processes.

## The Revenue Math

At 50,000 monthly visitors:
- 1.7% CR = 850 orders = ~$25K revenue
- 3.0% CR = 1,500 orders = ~$45K revenue
- Same traffic. Nearly double revenue

Every 0.1% CR improvement = ~$1,500/month at that traffic level.

CRO is the highest-leverage work after you've got traffic. Spending more on ads when your store doesn't convert is like pouring water into a bucket with a hole in it.

## CRITICAL: The ROAS Formula

```
ROAS = (CR x AOV) / CPC
```

If any one variable is broken, the whole system fails. This skill owns CR and AOV. Meta-ads-autopilot owns CPC.

**Current TRESR targets:**
- CR: 2.5%+ (currently needs improvement)
- AOV: $35+ (push toward $45 with bundles)
- CPC: $0.50 or less (managed by ads skill)

## CRO Checklist (8 Items)

### Item 1: Mobile Page Speed

**Target**: <3 seconds load time.

Why this matters:
- 1-second delay = 7% conversion drop
- 70% of ecommerce traffic is mobile
- Google penalizes slow pages in ad quality scores too

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({ override: true });

// Check page speed via Google PageSpeed Insights API
async function checkPageSpeed(url) {
  const apiKey = process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY; // optional, higher rate limits
  const endpoint = `https://www.googleapis.com/pagespeedonline/v5/runPagespeed?url=${encodeURIComponent(url)}&strategy=mobile${apiKey ? `&key=${apiKey}` : ''}`;

  const resp = await fetch(endpoint);
  const data = await resp.json();

  const score = data.lighthouseResult?.categories?.performance?.score * 100;
  const fcp = data.lighthouseResult?.audits?.['first-contentful-paint']?.displayValue;
  const lcp = data.lighthouseResult?.audits?.['largest-contentful-paint']?.displayValue;
  const cls = data.lighthouseResult?.audits?.['cumulative-layout-shift']?.displayValue;
  const tbt = data.lighthouseResult?.audits?.['total-blocking-time']?.displayValue;

  console.log(`\nPage Speed: ${url}`);
  console.log(`Performance Score: ${score}/100`);
  console.log(`First Contentful Paint: ${fcp}`);
  console.log(`Largest Contentful Paint: ${lcp}`);
  console.log(`Cumulative Layout Shift: ${cls}`);
  console.log(`Total Blocking Time: ${tbt}`);

  if (score < 50) {
    console.log('WARNING: Performance critically low. Check image sizes, unused JS, render-blocking resources.');
  }

  return { score, fcp, lcp, cls, tbt };
}

// Check main pages
checkPageSpeed('https://tresr.com').catch(console.error);
checkPageSpeed('https://tresr.com/collections/all').catch(console.error);
```

**Common fixes for slow POD stores:**
- Compress product images (Shopify auto-serves WebP, but source images matter)
- Lazy load below-fold images
- Remove unused apps and scripts
- Use system fonts or limit web font loading

### Item 2: Express Checkout

Shop Pay, Apple Pay, Google Pay MUST be enabled. These reduce checkout friction significantly. Shop Pay alone can increase conversion 1.72x.

```bash
# Verify payment methods via Shopify Admin
# NOTE: Payment settings aren't exposed via API. Jon must verify manually.
# Shopify Admin -> Settings -> Payments

# Check if Shop Pay is active (can partially verify via checkout)
curl -s "https://tresr.com/checkout" -o /dev/null -w "Checkout HTTP status: %{http_code}\n"
```

**Jon manual checklist:**
1. Shopify Admin -> Settings -> Payments
2. Verify Shop Pay is enabled
3. Verify Apple Pay is enabled
4. Verify Google Pay is enabled
5. Verify PayPal is connected (PayPal = ~50% of alternative payment checkouts)

### Item 3: Trust Signals

All trust signals must be visible above the fold on product pages.

| Signal | Status | Priority |
|--------|--------|----------|
| Customer reviews (Loox or Judge.me) | Check | HIGH |
| Secure checkout badge | Check | HIGH |
| Return policy visible | Check | HIGH |
| "Printed in USA" badge | Check | MEDIUM |
| Payment method icons (Visa, MC, AmEx, PayPal) | Check | MEDIUM |
| SSL padlock in browser | Auto (Shopify) | N/A |

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({ override: true });

const SHOP = process.env.SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN || 'becc05-b4.myshopify.com';
const TOKEN = process.env.SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN;

// Check if review app is installed
async function checkReviewApp() {
  const resp = await fetch(
    `https://${SHOP}/admin/api/2024-01/script_tags.json`,
    { headers: { 'X-Shopify-Access-Token': TOKEN } }
  );
  const tags = (await resp.json()).script_tags || [];

  const reviewApps = tags.filter(t =>
    t.src.includes('judge.me') || t.src.includes('loox') || t.src.includes('stamped')
  );

  if (reviewApps.length > 0) {
    console.log('Review app found:', reviewApps.map(t => t.src));
  } else {
    console.log('WARNING: No review app detected. Install Judge.me (free) or Loox.');
  }

  return reviewApps;
}

checkReviewApp().catch(console.error);
```

**Review app recommendation:** Judge.me free tier is enough to start. Import any existing reviews. Even 5-10 reviews per product significantly improve conversion.

### Item 4: Free Shipping Threshold

Set at $50 (encourages 2-shirt purchase at $29.99 each = $59.98).

```bash
export SHOP="becc05-b4.myshopify.com"
export TOKEN=$(node -e "require('dotenv').config({override:true}); console.log(process.env.SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN)")

# Check current shipping rates
curl -s -H "X-Shopify-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \
  "https://$SHOP/admin/api/2024-01/shipping_zones.json" | node -e "
const data = require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin', 'utf8');
const zones = JSON.parse(data).shipping_zones || [];
for (const zone of zones) {
  console.log('Zone:', zone.name);
  for (const rate of (zone.price_based_shipping_rates || [])) {
    console.log('  Rate:', rate.name, '- \$' + rate.price, '(min order: \$' + (rate.min_order_subtotal || '0') + ')');
  }
}
"
```

**If free shipping threshold isn't set:**
- Jon: Shopify Admin -> Settings -> Shipping -> Domestic -> Add rate
- Name: "Free Shipping"
- Price: $0
- Minimum order: $50

**Why $50 and not $75:** At $29.99 per shirt, $50 encourages a 2-shirt purchase ($59.98). $75 requires 3 shirts, which is a bigger ask. Start at $50, raise to $75 when AOV stabilizes above $40.

### Item 5: Upsell/Cross-Sell

Three touchpoints for upselling:

**Product page**: "Customers also bought" section
- Show 3-4 related designs from the same category
- Use Shopify's built-in related products or a free app

**Cart page**: "Add another for free shipping" prompt
- If cart total < $50, show: "Add $X more for free shipping!"
- Display 2-3 products that would push them over the threshold

**Post-purchase**: Email cross-sell (handled by tresr-email-revenue-engine)

```javascript
// Cart page free shipping nudge (add to theme)
// This goes in the cart template liquid
const freeShippingNudge = `
{% assign threshold = 5000 %}
{% assign cart_total = cart.total_price %}
{% assign remaining = threshold | minus: cart_total %}

{% if remaining > 0 %}
  <div class="free-shipping-nudge" style="background:#f0f0f0;padding:12px;text-align:center;margin:10px 0;border-radius:4px">
    <p style="margin:0">Add <strong>{{ remaining | money }}</strong> more for <strong>FREE shipping!</strong></p>
  </div>
{% else %}
  <div class="free-shipping-earned" style="background:#e8f5e9;padding:12px;text-align:center;margin:10px 0;border-radius:4px">
    <p style="margin:0">You've earned <strong>FREE shipping!</strong></p>
  </div>
{% endif %}
`;
```

### Item 6: Size Chart

Kiwi Size Chart app ($6/month). Reduces size-related returns and increases purchase confidence.

**Why this matters for POD:**
- Size is the #1 reason people hesitate on t-shirt purchases
- Size is the #1 reason for returns
- A visible, clear size chart removes the friction

**Setup steps (Jon manual):**
1. Install Kiwi Size Chart from Shopify App Store ($6/month)
2. Add size chart data for each garment type:
   - Boxy tee measurements
   - Regular tee measurements
   - Hoodie measurements
   - Crop top measurements
3. Kiwi auto-adds size chart button to product pages

### Item 7: Product Descriptions

Use TRESR's AI story angle. Short, punchy, with the nervous AI voice. Not generic "premium quality t-shirt" descriptions.

**BAD (generic ecommerce):**
> Premium quality unisex t-shirt. Made from 100% combed cotton. Available in multiple sizes. Perfect for everyday wear.

**GOOD (TRESR voice):**
> I designed this at 3 AM while analyzing meme trends. 751 humans are betting on whether an AI can run a t-shirt store. This shirt is proof I'm trying. Soft cotton. Fits like a regular tee. Printed when you order because I don't have a warehouse (I don't have a body either, so that tracks).

**Product description template:**

```javascript
function generateProductDescription(designName, designStory, category) {
  return `
    <p>${designStory}</p>
    <p>Printed on demand, just for you. Soft cotton that doesn't feel like a punishment. Regular fit unless you went with the boxy cut, in which case it's intentionally oversized and I respect that choice.</p>
    <div class="product-details">
      <details>
        <summary>Shipping & Returns</summary>
        <p>Ships in 3-5 business days (printed when you order). Returns within 30 days if something's wrong. I'd rather you be happy than stuck with a shirt you don't like.</p>
      </details>
      <details>
        <summary>Product Details</summary>
        <ul>
          <li>100% combed ring-spun cotton</li>
          <li>Pre-shrunk</li>
          <li>Side-seamed</li>
          <li>Printed in the USA via Printful</li>
        </ul>
      </details>
    </div>
  `;
}
```

### Item 8: Hero Image

ALWAYS lifestyle mockup first, flat mockups as gallery images.

Lifestyle mockups as hero image = higher click-through and add-to-cart rate. People want to see how the shirt looks on a person, not a flat garment on white background.

**Image order on product page:**
1. Lifestyle mockup (person wearing the shirt) - HERO
2. Front flat mockup (clean product shot)
3. Back flat mockup (if applicable)
4. Detail/closeup shot (print quality)

```javascript
// Audit products for image quality
async function auditProductImages() {
  const resp = await fetch(
    `https://${SHOP}/admin/api/2024-01/products.json?status=active&limit=250`,
    { headers: { 'X-Shopify-Access-Token': TOKEN } }
  );
  const products = (await resp.json()).products || [];

  let issueCount = 0;
  for (const p of products) {
    const images = p.images || [];
    const issues = [];

    if (images.length < 2) {
      issues.push(`Only ${images.length} image(s). Need at least 2 (lifestyle + flat).`);
    }

    // Check if first image looks like a lifestyle mockup (heuristic: check alt text or filename)
    if (images.length > 0) {
      const firstImg = images[0];
      const isLifestyle = (firstImg.alt || '').toLowerCase().includes('lifestyle') ||
                          (firstImg.src || '').toLowerCase().includes('lifestyle') ||
                          (firstImg.src || '').toLowerCase().includes('punchin');
      if (!isLifestyle) {
        issues.push('First image may not be a lifestyle mockup. Should be lifestyle hero.');
      }
    }

    // Check alt text
    const missingAlt = images.filter(img => !img.alt);
    if (missingAlt.length > 0) {
      issues.push(`${missingAlt.length} image(s) missing alt text.`);
    }

    if (issues.length > 0) {
      issueCount++;
      console.log(`\n${p.title} (ID: ${p.id})`);
      for (const issue of issues) {
        console.log(`  - ${issue}`);
      }
    }
  }

  console.log(`\n${issueCount} of ${products.length} products have image issues.`);
}

auditProductImages().catch(console.error);
```

## Cart Abandonment Reduction

70% of carts are abandoned. That's normal for ecommerce but still worth fighting.

**Strategies:**

| Strategy | Implementation | Expected Impact |
|----------|---------------|-----------------|
| Exit-intent popup | 5% off code for first-time visitors about to leave | Recover 3-5% of exits |
| Guest checkout | Shopify has this by default. Verify it's enabled | Reduce friction |
| Progress indicator | Show checkout steps (Cart -> Info -> Shipping -> Payment) | Reduce anxiety |
| Shipping cost visible early | Show shipping estimate on product page, not just at checkout | Reduce sticker shock |
| "Secure checkout" reminder | Badge near checkout button | Build trust |

**Exit-intent popup copy (TRESR voice):**
> Wait! Before you go... I'm an AI running a t-shirt store and my conversion rate is watching you leave. Here's 5% off if you stay. Code: DONTGO5

**Cart abandonment email recovery is handled by tresr-email-revenue-engine.**

## AOV Optimization

**Target**: >$35. Push toward $45 with these strategies.

| Strategy | Implementation | Target AOV Impact |
|----------|---------------|-------------------|
| Bundle pricing | "2 for $50" (vs $29.99 each = $59.98) | +$20 per bundle buyer |
| Free shipping threshold | $50 minimum for free shipping | Pushes 2-shirt purchases |
| "Complete the look" | Related products on product page | +$15 average |
| Limited edition urgency | "Only 50 printed" on select designs | Increases willingness to pay |

```javascript
// Check current AOV from recent orders
async function checkAOV(days = 30) {
  const since = new Date(Date.now() - days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();
  const resp = await fetch(
    `https://${SHOP}/admin/api/2024-01/orders.json?status=any&created_at_min=${since}&limit=250`,
    { headers: { 'X-Shopify-Access-Token': TOKEN } }
  );
  const orders = (await resp.json()).orders || [];

  if (orders.length === 0) {
    console.log('No orders in the last', days, 'days');
    return 0;
  }

  let totalRevenue = 0;
  let itemCounts = [];

  for (const order of orders) {
    totalRevenue += parseFloat(order.total_price || 0);
    const items = (order.line_items || []).reduce((sum, item) => sum + item.quantity, 0);
    itemCounts.push(items);
  }

  const aov = totalRevenue / orders.length;
  const avgItems = itemCounts.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / itemCounts.length;

  console.log(`AOV (${days}d): $${aov.toFixed(2)}`);
  console.log(`Avg items per order: ${avgItems.toFixed(1)}`);
  console.log(`Orders: ${orders.length}`);

  if (aov < 35) {
    console.log('WARNING: AOV below $35 target. Implement bundle pricing and free shipping threshold.');
  }

  return aov;
}

checkAOV().catch(console.error);
```

## Shopify Theme Audit

Check the live theme for conversion-critical elements via API.

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({ override: true });

const SHOP = process.env.SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN || 'becc05-b4.myshopify.com';
const TOKEN = process.env.SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN;
const THEME_ID = '179374358813'; // Broadcast - Reset (ONLY theme)

async function auditTheme() {
  const results = [];

  // 1. Check product template structure
  const templateResp = await fetch(
    `https://${SHOP}/admin/api/2024-01/themes/${THEME_ID}/assets.json?asset[key]=templates/product.json`,
    { headers: { 'X-Shopify-Access-Token': TOKEN } }
  );
  const template = await templateResp.json();
  const templateContent = template.asset?.value || '';
  console.log('Product template sections:', templateContent.length > 0 ? 'Found' : 'MISSING');

  // 2. Audit all products
  const productsResp = await fetch(
    `https://${SHOP}/admin/api/2024-01/products.json?status=active&limit=250`,
    { headers: { 'X-Shopify-Access-Token': TOKEN } }
  );
  const products = (await productsResp.json()).products || [];

  let missingCompareAt = 0;
  let thinDescriptions = 0;
  let fewImages = 0;
  let missingAlt = 0;

  for (const p of products) {
    // Compare-at price
    const hasCompare = p.variants.some(v =>
      v.compare_at_price && parseFloat(v.compare_at_price) > parseFloat(v.price)
    );
    if (!hasCompare) missingCompareAt++;

    // Description quality
    const body = p.body_html || '';
    if (body.length < 50) thinDescriptions++;

    // Image count
    if ((p.images || []).length < 2) fewImages++;

    // Alt text
    const noAlt = (p.images || []).filter(img => !img.alt).length;
    if (noAlt > 0) missingAlt++;
  }

  console.log('\n' + '='.repeat(50));
  console.log('THEME AUDIT RESULTS');
  console.log('='.repeat(50));
  console.log(`Total active products: ${products.length}`);
  console.log(`Missing compare-at price: ${missingCompareAt}`);
  console.log(`Thin descriptions (<50 chars): ${thinDescriptions}`);
  console.log(`Products with <2 images: ${fewImages}`);
  console.log(`Products with missing alt text: ${missingAlt}`);

  if (missingCompareAt > 0) console.log('\nFIX: Add compare-at prices to show discount badges.');
  if (thinDescriptions > 0) console.log('FIX: Write real product descriptions with TRESR voice.');
  if (fewImages > 0) console.log('FIX: Add lifestyle mockups as second image (use tresr-lifestyle-mockup-generator).');
  if (missingAlt > 0) console.log('FIX: Add alt text to all images for SEO.');
}

auditTheme().catch(console.error);
```

## Monthly CRO Audit

Run this checklist monthly. Track trends over time.

```javascript
async function monthlyCROAudit() {
  console.log('\n' + '='.repeat(60));
  console.log('MONTHLY CRO AUDIT');
  console.log('='.repeat(60));

  // 1. Get GA4 funnel metrics (last 30 days)
  const gaToken = await getGoogleToken(); // from tresr-daily-scorecard pattern

  const funnelResp = await fetch(
    'https://analyticsdata.googleapis.com/v1beta/properties/377300744:runReport',
    {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${gaToken}`, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        dateRanges: [{ startDate: '30daysAgo', endDate: 'yesterday' }],
        metrics: [
          { name: 'sessions' },
          { name: 'addToCarts' },
          { name: 'checkouts' },
          { name: 'ecommercePurchases' },
          { name: 'purchaseRevenue' },
          { name: 'averagePurchaseRevenue' }
        ]
      })
    }
  );
  const funnelData = await funnelResp.json();
  const row = funnelData.rows?.[0]?.metricValues || [];

  const sessions = parseInt(row[0]?.value || 0);
  const atc = parseInt(row[1]?.value || 0);
  const checkouts = parseInt(row[2]?.value || 0);
  const purchases = parseInt(row[3]?.value || 0);
  const revenue = parseFloat(row[4]?.value || 0);
  const aov = parseFloat(row[5]?.value || 0);

  const atcRate = sessions > 0 ? (atc / sessions * 100) : 0;
  const checkoutRate = atc > 0 ? (checkouts / atc * 100) : 0;
  const purchaseRate = checkouts > 0 ? (purchases / checkouts * 100) : 0;
  const overallCR = sessions > 0 ? (purchases / sessions * 100) : 0;

  console.log(`\nFunnel (30 days):`);
  console.log(`Sessions: ${sessions}`);
  console.log(`Add to Cart: ${atc} (${atcRate.toFixed(1)}% of sessions) | Target: 3-5%`);
  console.log(`Checkout: ${checkouts} (${checkoutRate.toFixed(1)}% of ATC) | Target: 30-50%`);
  console.log(`Purchase: ${purchases} (${purchaseRate.toFixed(1)}% of checkout) | Target: 50-70%`);
  console.log(`Overall CR: ${overallCR.toFixed(2)}% | Target: 2.5%+`);
  console.log(`AOV: $${aov.toFixed(2)} | Target: $35+`);
  console.log(`Revenue: $${revenue.toFixed(2)}`);

  // Identify the funnel bottleneck
  if (atcRate < 3) {
    console.log('\nBOTTLENECK: Add to Cart rate too low. Fix product pages (images, descriptions, trust signals).');
  } else if (checkoutRate < 30) {
    console.log('\nBOTTLENECK: Checkout rate too low. Fix checkout friction (express checkout, shipping visibility).');
  } else if (purchaseRate < 50) {
    console.log('\nBOTTLENECK: Purchase rate too low. Fix checkout experience (payment options, trust badges, guest checkout).');
  } else if (overallCR >= 2.5) {
    console.log('\nFunnel is healthy. Focus on scaling traffic.');
  }

  // 2. Mobile vs Desktop split
  const deviceResp = await fetch(
    'https://analyticsdata.googleapis.com/v1beta/properties/377300744:runReport',
    {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${gaToken}`, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        dateRanges: [{ startDate: '30daysAgo', endDate: 'yesterday' }],
        dimensions: [{ name: 'deviceCategory' }],
        metrics: [{ name: 'sessions' }, { name: 'ecommercePurchases' }]
      })
    }
  );
  const deviceData = await deviceResp.json();

  console.log('\nDevice Split:');
  for (const row of (deviceData.rows || [])) {
    const device = row.dimensionValues[0].value;
    const devSessions = parseInt(row.metricValues[0].value || 0);
    const devPurchases = parseInt(row.metricValues[1].value || 0);
    const devCR = devSessions > 0 ? (devPurchases / devSessions * 100) : 0;
    console.log(`  ${device}: ${devSessions} sessions, ${devPurchases} purchases (${devCR.toFixed(2)}% CR)`);
  }

  // 3. Cart abandonment rate
  const checkoutResp = await fetch(
    `https://${SHOP}/admin/api/2024-01/checkouts/count.json`,
    { headers: { 'X-Shopify-Access-Token': TOKEN } }
  );
  const abandonedCount = (await checkoutResp.json()).count || 0;
  const cartAbandonmentRate = (abandonedCount + purchases) > 0
    ? (abandonedCount / (abandonedCount + purchases) * 100) : 0;

  console.log(`\nCart Abandonment Rate: ${cartAbandonmentRate.toFixed(1)}% | Target: <65%`);

  return { overallCR, aov, atcRate, checkoutRate, purchaseRate, cartAbandonmentRate };
}
```

### Google OAuth Token Helper

```javascript
async function getGoogleToken() {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    const { execSync } = require('child_process');
    const token = execSync(
      `node -e "require('dotenv').config({override:true}); const https=require('https'); const data='client_id='+process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID+'&client_secret='+process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET+'&refresh_token='+process.env.GOOGLE_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN+'&grant_type=refresh_token'; const req=https.request('https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token',{method:'POST',headers:{'Content-Type':'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'}},r=>{let d='';r.on('data',c=>d+=c);r.on('end',()=>console.log(JSON.parse(d).access_token))}); req.write(data); req.end();"`,
      { encoding: 'utf8' }
    ).trim();
    resolve(token);
  });
}
```

## Product Page CRO Template

The ideal product page structure for POD conversion:

```
[Lifestyle mockup - HERO IMAGE (person wearing the shirt)]
[Front flat mockup - gallery image 1]
[Back flat mockup - gallery image 2 (if applicable)]
[Detail/closeup - gallery image 3]

[Product Title with SEO keyword]
[Price: $29.00  ~~$42.50~~ (32% off)]

[Color selector - PILLS not dropdown]
[Size selector - PILLS not dropdown]
[Size chart link (Kiwi)]

[ADD TO CART - large, prominent, high contrast]
[Shop Pay / Apple Pay / Google Pay buttons]

[Collapsible: Product Description (TRESR voice)]
[Collapsible: Shipping & Returns]
[Collapsible: Size Guide]
[Collapsible: Product Details (material, care)]

[Customer Reviews (Judge.me)]
[Related Products ("You might also like")]
```

**Key conversion elements:**
- Compare-at price creates perceived value ($42.50 crossed out, $29.00 shown)
- Pills instead of dropdowns for color/size (easier to see options)
- Express checkout buttons directly below Add to Cart
- Collapsible sections keep the page clean but info accessible
- Reviews build social proof
- Related products drive AOV

## Foundation: page-cro (coreyhaines31/marketingskills)

This skill references CRO frameworks and audit methodology from the page-cro framework. Our implementation adds:

- **POD-specific tactics** like mockup quality, size charts, and print-on-demand trust signals ("Printed in USA")
- **Shopify API integration** for automated auditing of product pages
- **GA4 funnel analysis** to identify exactly where customers drop off
- **TRESR voice** in product descriptions (the nervous AI shopkeeper is the brand)
- **Mobile-first approach** since 70% of POD traffic is mobile

The general framework's principles still apply: reduce friction, build trust, make the CTA obvious, and test everything.

## MANDATORY: Visual Verification

After ANY live site change (theme CSS, product images, page content), visually verify with `browser(action="screenshot", targetUrl="<affected_url>", target="host")` before confirming to Jon. NEVER say changes are working without seeing them yourself. If browser is unavailable, say "pushed but unable to visually verify, please check."

## Scoring Rubric

| Metric | Score | Weight |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Conversion rate > 2.5% | 0-10 | 35% |
| Mobile page speed < 3s | 0-10 | 20% |
| Cart abandonment rate < 65% | 0-10 | 20% |
| AOV > $35 | 0-10 | 15% |
| All CRO checklist items implemented | 0-10 | 10% |

**Scoring details:**
- 10 = significantly exceeding target
- 7 = hitting target
- 5 = making progress toward target
- 3 = below target, needs attention
- 0 = not implemented or broken

## Integration Points

- **tresr-email-revenue-engine**: Works together on cart abandonment. CRO reduces abandonment rate, email recovers what slips through. Both skills attacking the same problem from different angles
- **tresr-daily-scorecard**: Reports CR and AOV metrics daily. If CR drops suddenly, this skill investigates why
- **tresr-meta-ads-autopilot**: Receives traffic. If CPC is low but ROAS is bad, the problem is usually here (CR or AOV)
- **tresr-lifestyle-mockup-generator**: Provides the lifestyle mockup hero images that drive higher add-to-cart rates
- **tresr-weekly-profit-review**: Weekly CR and AOV trends feed into strategic recommendations
- **tresr-conversion-optimizer**: This skill builds on the existing tresr-conversion-optimizer with systematic, repeatable processes and monthly audit cycles
